answer:
The steady-state solution is found by setting \(y[n] = C\) constant, giving \(C - \frac{5}{6}C + \frac{1}{6}C = 2\), so \(C = 6\).  
The homogeneous solution solves \(y[n] - \frac{5}{6}y[n-1] + \frac{1}{6}y[n-2] = 0\), with characteristic roots \(\frac{1}{2}\) and \(\frac{1}{3}\), so the form is \(A(1/2)^n + B(1/3)^n\).  
Using initial conditions \(y[-1]=1\) and \(y[-2]=-7\) yields \(A = -1\) and \(B = -1\), giving \(y[n] = 6 - (1/2)^n - (1/3)^n\).

\boxed{B}
